title: pavonia racemosa
intro: Pavonia racemosa, also known as rock rose or rose mallow, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States, Mexico, and parts of Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Pavonia racemosa include its ability to treat various conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Pavonia racemosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or salve, and is also used in herbal preparations to make creams, ointments, and other topical applications.
